title: ceratotheca integribracteata subsp. elliptica
intro: Ceratotheca integribracteata subsp. elliptica, also known as the 'forest needle flower', is a plant species within the Pedaliaceae family. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, with roots and stems being used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as relieving pain and reducing inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in hot water for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
